paper_title: Microplastic Deposition on the Irish Continental Shelf

methods.description: Selection of study areas on the Irish continental shelf to examine microplastic deposition in remote and proximal oceanographic environments.

steps.description: Two main study areas on the Irish continental shelf were selected to examine microplastic deposition in remote and proximal oceanographic environments.
steps.searchable_summary: select study areas on the Irish continental shelf for microplastic deposition examination

methods.description: Collection of sediment samples from remote and proximal locations to analyze microplastic deposition.

steps.description: Sediments from remote locations were retrieved during a R.V. Celtic Explorer cruise (CE14019) on the 15th and 16th of February 2014.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve sediment samples from remote locations during R.V. Celtic Explorer cruise

steps.description: The Aran Grounds fishery and the fishery within Galway Bay's North Sound were sampled during a R.V. Celtic Voyager cruise (CV15025) between the 7th and 9th of October 2015.
steps.searchable_summary: sample Aran Grounds fishery and Galway Bay's North Sound during R.V. Celtic Voyager cruise

steps.description: For each location, two core liners of 66 mm diameter were carefully placed into a reineck box corer in order to sample and preserve the water-sediment interface with both replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: use core liners and reineck box corer to sample and preserve water-sediment interface

steps.description: All sampled cores were subsequently transferred to the National University of Ireland, Galway (NUIG) and refrigerated in an upright position until processed.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer and refrigerate sampled cores at NUIG
